summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Types of Rocks (Igneous, Sedimentary, Metamorphic)
summary_sections.content_html:
All rocks belong to one of three major categories based on how they form:

  • Igneous rocks form from the cooling and solidification of magma or lava.
    • Intrusive (plutonic): cool slowly underground → large crystals (e.g., granite)
    • Extrusive (volcanic): cool quickly at the surface → small/no crystals (e.g., basalt, obsidian)
  • Sedimentary rocks form from compaction and cementation of sediments, or from chemical/biological processes.
    • Clastic: made of rock fragments (sandstone, shale)
    • Chemical: precipitated from solution (limestone, rock salt)
    • Organic: from remains of organisms (coal, some limestone)
  • Metamorphic rocks form when existing rocks are transformed by heat and/or pressure without melting.
    • Foliated: layered appearance (slate, gneiss)
    • Non-foliated: no layers (marble, quartzite)
